blocks.body: Past Simple: przeczenia — didn't + V1 w przeczeniach w przeszłości.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"W przeczeniach Past Simple używamy did not albo didn't, a główny czasownik wraca do V1.","rules":[{"label":"did not / didn't","color":"emerald","text":"Forma pełna i krótka znaczą to samo.","example":"I did not call. / I didn't call."},{"label":"Back to V1","color":"blue","text":"Po did główny czasownik wraca do formy podstawowej.","example":"She didn't go. / We didn't see it."},{"label":"Same for all subjects","color":"amber","text":"Forma didn't jest taka sama dla każdego podmiotu.","example":"I didn't work. / He didn't work. / They didn't work."}]}

{"title":"1. Podstawowe modele","intro":"Po did zawsze używaj V1.","items":[{"label":"I / you / we / they","title":"didn't + V1","subtitle":"I didn't call. / They didn't stay."},{"label":"he / she / it","title":"didn't + V1","subtitle":"He didn't go. / She didn't open it."},{"label":"Full form","title":"did not + V1","subtitle":"We did not watch the film."},{"label":"Contrast","title":"worked → didn't work","subtitle":"W przeczeniu forma przeszła znika."}]}

{"title":"2. Po co są przeczenia","sections":[{"label":"Czynność się nie wydarzyła","color":"emerald","description":"Pokaż, że czynność nie wydarzyła się w przeszłości.","examples":[{"en":"I didn't see Anna yesterday.","ua":"Czynność się nie wydarzyła."},{"en":"They didn't finish the task.","ua":"Negatywne wydarzenie w przeszłości."}]},{"label":"Kontrast","color":"blue","description":"Porównuj to, co się wydarzyło, z tym, co się nie wydarzyło.","examples":[{"en":"She cooked dinner, but she didn't make dessert.","ua":"Jedna czynność się wydarzyła, druga nie."},{"en":"We visited Rome, but we didn't go to Naples.","ua":"Wyraźny kontrast w przeszłości."}]},{"label":"Jedna forma dla wszystkich","color":"amber","description":"Nie musisz zmieniać did zależnie od podmiotu.","examples":[{"en":"He didn't work yesterday.","ua":"Ta sama forma co z I albo they."},{"en":"They didn't work yesterday.","ua":"didn't się nie zmienia."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Twierdzenie i przeczenie","intro":"Porównaj model pozytywny i negatywny.","rows":[{"en":"I worked yesterday.","ua":"I didn't work yesterday.","note":"worked → didn't work"},{"en":"She went home early.","ua":"She didn't go home early.","note":"went → didn't go"},{"en":"We saw him after class.","ua":"We didn't see him after class.","note":"saw → didn't see"}],"warning":"Po didn't nie używaj went, saw ani formy z -ed."}

{"title":"4. Typowe błędy","items":[{"label":"Błąd 1","color":"rose","title":"Używanie V2 po didn't.","wrong":"❌ He didn't went home.","right":"✅ He didn't go home."},{"label":"Błąd 2","color":"amber","title":"Zostawianie -ed po didn't.","wrong":"❌ We didn't played well.","right":"✅ We didn't play well."},{"label":"Błąd 3","color":"sky","title":"Budowanie przeczenia bez did.","wrong":"❌ She not go to school yesterday.","right":"✅ She didn't go to school yesterday."}]}

{"title":"5. Krótkie podsumowanie","items":["Przeczenie w Past Simple = didn't + V1.","did not i didn't znaczą to samo.","Po did / didn't główny czasownik zawsze zostaje w V1.","Forma didn't jest taka sama dla każdego podmiotu."]}
